decks.notes: The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with quick and aggressive creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Cori-Steel Cutter, backed up by efficient burn spells such as Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board. The deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain tempo with cards like Mishra's Bauble and Expressive Iteration, while also utilizing pump spells like Mutagenic Growth and Violent Urge to maximize damage output. The deck is focused on being proactive and closing out the game quickly before the opponent can establish their own game plan.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly fill up the graveyard with powerful creatures, then bring them back onto the battlefield using spells like Unearth and Persist. This allows the player to create a powerful board presence and overwhelm their opponent with hard-hitting creatures like Archon of Cruelty and Emperor of Bones. Faithless Looting and Thoughseize help to sculpt the player's hand and disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Fatal Push and Spell Pierce provide removal and protection. The deck aims to win through a combination of strong creatures and disruptive spells.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly ramp up mana and use powerful combo cards like Basking Broodscale, Devourer of Destiny, and Malevolent Rumble to overwhelm the opponent with strong creatures and devastating effects. By utilizing Eldrazi Temple, Urza's Saga, and Grove of the Burnwillows, the deck aims to accelerate its game plan and outpace the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Ancient Stirrings and Blade of the Bloodchief helps to find key pieces and enhance the overall strategy. The deck also includes some control elements like Ugin's Labyrinth and Kozilek's Command to disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The main focus of this UW Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's game plan through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage engines. With a variety of control elements such as Teferi, Time Raveler, Prismatic Ending, and Wrath of the Skies, the deck aims to stall the game while setting up win conditions like Narset, Parter of Veils or Monumental Henge. Additionally, the inclusion of a companion, Kaheera, the Orphanguard, adds an additional creature threat and synergizes well with the deck's game plan. Overall, the key concept of this deck is to control the game and outvalue the opponent.

This deck focuses on quickly playing powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with efficient and hard-hitting threats, while also using cards like Eternal Scourge and Serum Powder to maintain card advantage and consistency. With a mix of aggressive creatures and disruptive elements, this deck aims to apply pressure early and consistently to secure victories in Modern 1 vs 1 matches.

The deck focuses on using reanimation spells like Goryo's Vengeance to cheat powerful creatures like Griselbrand into play at instant speed. This is supported by control elements like Thoughtseize and Force of Negation to disrupt the opponent's game plan. Additionally, cards like Ephemerate and Solitude provide interaction and protection. The deck aims to create explosive plays by quickly bringing back creatures from the graveyard and overwhelming the opponent with powerful threats, while also being able to control the game and handle opposing threats efficiently.

This deck aims to quickly assemble the Song of Creation combo, utilizing cards like Mox Amber and Mishra's Bauble to generate extra mana and card draw. Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and Emry, Lurker of the Loch provide additional card advantage and recursion, while Urza's Saga helps to tutor for key pieces. The deck also includes win conditions like Grapeshot and Jace, Wielder of Mysteries. Overall, the deck focuses on explosive combos and quick victories through efficient card draw and mana generation.

The deck revolves around aggressive plays using low-cost creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. It utilizes efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart to control the board while creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler deal damage quickly. The deck also includes card draw and selection spells like Preordain and Expressive Iteration to maintain consistency and find key cards. Overall, the strategy is to maintain pressure, control the board, and finish off the opponent with a flurry of spells and creature attacks.

The main focus of this Jeskai Control deck is to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy through counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage provided by cards like Teferi, Time Raveler and Snapcaster Mage. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Teferi, Hero of Dominaria and March of Otherworldly Light to close out the game. By utilizing a combination of control elements and card advantage, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and slowly gain control of the game until victory is achieved.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a mix of removal spells, counterspells, and card advantage. It aims to protect its key threats like Stoneforge Mystic and Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Teferi, Time Raveler and Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student provide additional card advantage and utility, while Kaldra Compleat and Meteor Sword offer a powerful win condition. The deck also includes some versatile cards like Prismatic Ending and Ephemerate to deal with a variety of threats. Overall, it is a versatile midrange control deck that can adapt to different matchups.

The main focus of this deck is to generate mana quickly and efficiently using cards like Ruby Medallion, Desperate Ritual, and Pyretic Ritual, while also cycling through the deck and finding combo pieces with cards like Ral, Monsoon Mage, Wish, and Reckless Impulse. The deck aims to storm off and finish the game with a massive Grapeshot or through other means like Valakut Awakening or Ral's abilities. The deck also includes cards like Gemstone Caverns and Past in Flames for extra ramp and recursion, making it a fast and explosive combo deck in Modern.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact creatures and spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent with cheap and efficient threats. By leveraging cards like Mox Opal and Urza's Saga, along with powerful artifacts like Arcbound Ravager and Engineered Explosives, the deck aims to aggressively deploy threats while disrupting the opponent's game plan. Additionally, the deck includes a variety of utility artifacts and spells to further enhance its aggro strategy and maximize its efficiency in a fast-paced Modern 1 vs 1 format.

The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with fast and aggressive creature plays, supported by powerful instant and sorcery spells. The deck utilizes a mix of red and white cards to efficiently deal damage to the opponent while maintaining board control. By utilizing cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Guide of Souls, along with burn spells like Galvanic Discharge and Goblin Bombardment, the deck aims to quickly finish off the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize. Additionally, the deck includes cards like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah and Haliya, Guided by Light for added support and resilience.

The deck aims to win through mill, forcing the opponent to draw cards from an empty library and lose the game. It utilizes cards like Hedron Crab, Ruin Crab, and Archive Trap to mill the opponent's deck quickly while also disrupting their game plan with counterspells like Drown in the Loch. Tasha's Hideous Laughter can exile a large portion of the opponent's library, putting them on a clock to find answers before they run out of cards. The deck also includes ways to disrupt the opponent's graveyard and draw cards to keep the mill engine going.

This Mardu Aggro deck focuses on generating a lot of small tokens quickly and using them in combination with sacrifice effects to deal damage to the opponent. The deck also includes powerful planeswalkers like Ajani, Nacatl Pariah to create more token creatures and provide additional value. The inclusion of cards like Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Goblin Bombardment allows for direct damage to be dealt to the opponent, putting pressure on them to respond quickly to the swarm of creatures being generated. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a fast, aggressive strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to put pressure on the opponent quickly and efficiently by playing aggressive creatures and burn spells to deal damage as quickly as possible, aiming to win the game before the opponent can stabilize. The deck utilizes cards that can deal direct damage to the opponent, as well as creatures with haste to attack right away. By constantly applying pressure and forcing the opponent to react, the deck aims to win the game through sheer aggression and speed.
